chunk_summary.idea_trajectory_justification: The group continues to brainstorm increasingly complex and novel models, such as painting neural pathways, perturbing complex communities, and transplanting in vitro cells back into in vivo systems.
chunk_summary.collective_engagement_justification: Non-speaking participants (Will, Rui, Judith) show visible head nods and smiles, and there is a moment of shared laughter, indicating consistent engagement despite some participants being less visibly reactive.
chunk_summary.cross_disciplinary_bridging_description: Connecting in vitro stem cell models with in vivo animal transplantation.
chunk_summary.problem_specificity_justification: The group is articulating specific research questions, such as whether different types of enteric neurons respond differently to microbial metabolites, and proposing specific models to test this.
chunk_summary.decision_crystallization_justification: The group has identified shared interests in complex in vitro/in vivo models (like the in vitro vagus nerve or transplantation) but has not yet agreed on a single specific project to pursue.
chunk_summary.shared_vision_quote: if we want to build a new model
chunk_summary.risk_enthusiasm_quote: That would be an amazing in vitro system.
chunk_summary.funding_reference_description: Judith explicitly mentions 'your proposal', referring to the grant proposal they are supposed to be developing.
chunk_summary.notable_observation: The group reached a moment of shared appreciative laughter and enthusiasm around the highly ambitious idea of building an 'in vitro vagus nerve', which sparked a creative leap into transplanting in vitro cells back into an in vivo system.

ideas_currently_on_table: Painting neural pathways layer by layer
chunk_summaries: The group brainstormed various in vitro and ex vivo models to study gut-brain communication, specifically focusing on how microbial metabolites interact with the enteric nervous system. Faranak Fattahi detailed microfluidic approaches, while Xin Qi proposed a screening platform using a bacteria library. The tone was highly collaborative and exploratory, ending with multiple complex modeling ideas on the table but no final decision.

The group continued to explore different modeling approaches, debating the trade-offs between top-down complex in vivo systems and bottom-up reductionist in vitro models. Xin Qi introduced an AI-based approach to identify top candidate molecules, while Lisa Osborne synthesized the discussion by noting that every model has caveats. The tone remained highly collaborative and exploratory, ending the chunk still in a divergent phase without a finalized project direction.

Rui Zhang proposed a novel method of 'painting' neural pathways layer by layer, which led to a discussion on the trade-offs of reductionist in vitro models. Will Ludington suggested perturbing complex microbial communities with specific metabolites, an approach Sarkis Mazmanian strongly supported. The chunk culminated in a moment of shared enthusiasm and laughter when Faranak Fattahi and Judith Eisen proposed the highly ambitious idea of building an 'in vitro vagus nerve' and transplanting in vitro cells back into in vivo systems, leaving the group in a highly creative, divergent state.
